title: Spec part
description: One normative section of the wacrg spec. Parts are small YAML files that compile into a single ever-scrolling spec page; each also renders as its own API-reference-style lookup page. A part's id is its permanent, citable reference.

id.description: Stable, globally-unique id; matches the file name. The permanent reference for this part.

code.description: Small stable annotation id (e.g. ENC-04). A flavor marks its implementation site in source with a `wacrg:<code>` comment; a script clones the source, finds the comment, and attaches a commit blame/permalink.

category.description: Top-level spec division; matches the parent directory under spec/.

order.description: Sort key within the category on the compiled spec page (lower first).

summary.description: One-paragraph statement of what this part specifies.

normative.description: The normative requirement text (RFC 2119 MUST/SHOULD/MAY), precise enough to implement. Markdown allowed.

findings.description: Supporting detail and what is known. Markdown allowed.

parent.description: Id of the parent part this one nests under in its category (e.g. mlow-rangecoder's parent is mlow). Must resolve to a part in the same category.

requires.description: Part ids this part depends on (each must resolve to another spec part).

discovered_by.description: Contributor id credited with first reverse-engineering this part (must match spec/contributors/).

implementations.description: Current in-the-wild implementation status across flavors (light; not a full code map).

since.description: Spec version this part was introduced or last materially changed.

commits.description: Commit SHAs in the flavor's repo that implement/changed this part. Each renders as its own commit link.
